title: Roving Tabindex Toolbar
description:
Build an accessible **toolbar** component that implements the roving tabindex pattern for arrow key navigation between tool buttons, with proper grouping and ARIA.

### Requirements

- Toolbar container has `role="toolbar"` and `aria-label`
- Only one button in the toolbar is in the tab order at a time (`tabIndex={0}`); all others have `tabIndex={-1}`
- **Left/Right arrow** keys move focus between buttons
- Arrow keys wrap around (last → first, first → last)
- **Home** key moves to first button, **End** key moves to last
- Buttons that are disabled (`aria-disabled="true"`) are skipped during arrow navigation
- Support button groups separated by a visual divider
- Each button has a tooltip shown on focus/hover via `aria-describedby`
- The toolbar remembers which button was last focused (roving focus persists)

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Toolbar | `toolbar` |
| Tool button | `tool-{id}` |
| Output display | `output` |
constraints:
- Must implement roving tabindex (not aria-activedescendant)
- Only one tabIndex={0} button at any time
- Disabled buttons must be skippable during keyboard nav
- Must handle groups seamlessly (arrow keys cross group boundaries)
- Tab key should leave the toolbar entirely (not cycle through buttons)
hints: Maintain a flat array of all non-disabled button refs. Track the current index. On arrow key, update the index and call focus().
For the roving tabindex: set tabIndex={0} on the button at `currentIndex` and tabIndex={-1} on all others.
To skip disabled buttons, write a helper that steps forward/backward until it finds a non-disabled button.
